page: Microsoft Azure E96_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Ev5 server family. Powered by the x86_64 architecture, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 96 virtual CPUs derived from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The E96_v5 achieves a multi-core stress-ng score of 93248.4 and a PassMark CPU score of 76593.39, indicating robust parallel processing capabilities. Its memory architecture demonstrates high throughput, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 5006.48 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 331.76 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test placing the instance in the top tier at 86888.24. Single-core performance remains moderate, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1713.0.

Given its high memory capacity and strong multi-threaded performance, the E96_v5 is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications. This is supported by a PassMark database operations score of 30848.73 and a Redis SET throughput of 87.1 million operations per second. It also handles medium-scale language model prompt processing efficiently, reaching 204.51 tokens per second on a 7B model. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing organizations to pay only for the external block storage they provision while leveraging the dense memory and compute resources of the host.
description: Microsoft Azure E96_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed, paired with 672.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 76593.39 and a top-tier encryption score of 86888.24. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 331.76 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. With a PassMark database score of 30848.73 and Redis SET performance of 87.1 million operations per second, this instance is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring high resource density without local storage overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor and 672 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 virtual CPUs and 672 GB of RAM designed for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

672.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 76593.39
Top-tier cryptographic performance and high memory read bandwidth
No bundled local storage, decoupling compute and storage costs
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
